channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Maya Hawke’s Life, Relationships and Career Highlights
item.description: Maya Hawke, born on 8th July 1998 in New York City, is an American actress and singer-songwriter who has gained attention for her diverse acting roles and music career. She is the daughter of famous a...

item.title: Kenya: Katiba Institute seeks court halt to proposed U.S.-linked Ebola quarantine facility; demands full contingency plans
item.description: The Katiba Institute has filed an urgent petition in Kenyan court to stop the planned establishment of a quarantine facility for American citizens and others exposed to Ebola or similar infectious dis...

item.title: Malabon city holds recycling drive where residents earn money from recyclable waste
item.description: Residents of Malabon City in the Philippines were given an opportunity to earn extra income by exchanging recyclable waste for cash during a city-led recycling collection drive. The initiative, titled...

item.title: CBS News Chief Bari Weiss Absent from Emmy Awards as Internal Tensions Surface
item.description: Bari Weiss, who became head of CBS News last October, was notably absent from the News & Documentary Emmy Awards in Manhattan, where '60 Minutes' was nominated for 10 awards. Weiss's absence drew atte...

item.title: Katiba Institute Challenges Kenyan Government Over Proposed Ebola Quarantine Facility
item.description: The Katiba Institute has filed an urgent petition in court to challenge the Kenyan government’s proposed plans to establish Ebola quarantine facilities, including for Americans. The petition, filed un...

item.title: Jannik Sinner exits French Open second round after falling ill against Cerundolo
item.description: Jannik Sinner, world number one and hot favourite for the French Open, suffered a shocking second-round defeat to Argentina's Juan Manuel Cerundolo in a five-set thriller. Struggling with illness and ...

item.title: DR Congo asks FIFA for refunds after fans blocked from World Cup travel due to Ebola restrictions
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) has officially asked FIFA to refund World Cup tickets for supporters who are unable to travel because of new international travel restrictions linked to an Ebola...

item.title: Tourist bus carrying Velankanni pilgrims overturns near Nellore, several injured
item.description: A private tourist bus carrying 43 pilgrims travelling to the Velankanni Church in Tamil Nadu met with an accident early on Thursday near Chintareddypalem in Nellore district, Andhra Pradesh. According...

item.title: Victor Ochei steps down from APC membership after years in the party
item.description: Former Speaker of the Delta State House of Assembly, Victor Ochei, has officially resigned his membership from the All Progressives Congress (APC), the ruling party in Nigeria. In a resignation letter...

item.title: Google Employee Charged With Insider Trading Over Polymarket Bets Using Confidential Search Data
item.description: Federal prosecutors in the Southern District of New York have charged Google employee Michele Spagnuolo with wire fraud, commodities fraud, and money laundering after allegedly using confidential inte...

item.title: Canada explores Saab aircraft purchase amid U.S. defence concerns
item.description: Canada has recently spent over $65 billion on defence and reached NATO’s 2 per cent of GDP spending target, yet the United States has expressed dissatisfaction with Canada’s defence commitments. On Ma...

item.title: Makonda appoints Haji Manara as spokesperson for Tanzania national football teams
item.description: The Minister for Information, Culture, Arts and Sports, Paul Makonda, has officially appointed Haji Manara as the new spokesperson for all Tanzania national football teams operating under the National...

item.title: Jannik Sinner loses to Juan Manuel Cerundolo in second round at French Open
item.description: Jannik Sinner, world No 1 and favourite for this year’s French Open, suffered a dramatic second-round defeat to Argentina's Juan Manuel Cerundolo, despite leading 6-3, 6-2, 5-1. Sinner described feeli...

item.title: Karseell Collagen and Argan Oil Hair Mask Available at Discount for Frizz Control
item.description: A popular collagen and argan oil hair mask from the brand Karseell is currently on sale at Amazon for $19.01, representing a 24% discount from its regular price of $24.86. The product has received str...

item.title: SARB raises repo rate to 7% amid rising inflation risks driven by oil and food prices
item.description: The South African Reserve Bank (SARB) has increased its main policy interest rate by 25 basis points to 7%, citing growing inflation risks linked to rising global oil prices and persistent pressure on...

item.title: PCCB stops illegal land transfer worth over Sh3 billion in Arusha
item.description: The Prevention and Combating of Corruption Bureau (PCCB) in Arusha has successfully stopped an illegal land transaction involving three plots valued at over Sh3 billion. The plots, located in Levolosi...

item.title: Fitch revises sovereign debt rules to clarify treatment of temporary payment pauses
item.description: This article explains how Fitch Ratings, one of the major global credit rating agencies, has updated its framework for assessing sovereign debt situations, especially when countries temporarily pause ...

item.title: Piki Lopez addresses Lopez family dispute during First Gen annual stockholders’ meeting
item.description: For the first time since the Lopez cousins' public feud began in March, Federico “Piki” Lopez appeared at First Gen Corporation’s virtual annual stockholders’ meeting on May 28, 2026. During the sessi...

item.title: Update on South Africa’s ‘KFC couple’ seven years later
item.description: Seven years after becoming a viral sensation in South Africa, Hector and Nonhlanhla Mkansi, widely known as the ‘KFC couple’, are still together and continuing their life as a family of five. The coup...

item.title: NDP Leader Avi Lewis distinguishes between Alberta and Quebec separatist movements
item.description: OTTAWA — NDP Leader Avi Lewis clarified that the Sherbrooke declaration, a foundational policy recognizing Quebec’s right to self-determination, does not extend to Alberta. Lewis dismissed Alberta's s...
